readme.purpose: Annual inflation rate schedule. This rate simultaneously: (1) inflates withdrawal targets from withdrawal_schedule.json so your take-home maintains purchasing power, (2) adjusts tax brackets each year so bracket thresholds grow with inflation, (3) converts between nominal and real dollars in results. Fed long-run target is ~2.0%. Recent 2022-2024 experience was 4-9%.
readme.years: Year-relative range. Year 1 = current_age + 1 (the first simulation year). Use '1-30' for a flat assumption or '1-10', '11-30' for a stepped assumption. Years are simulation-relative — not ages — because inflation describes the economic environment during the simulation, not a life-stage event.
readme.rate_pct: Annual inflation rate as a percentage. E.g. 2.3 = 2.3% per year, compounded. A higher rate means your withdrawal targets grow faster in nominal terms — but your real purchasing power target stays constant.
readme.format_note: This file intentionally uses year-relative format (not age-based). Inflation and shocks describe economic conditions during the simulation period, independent of the person's age.
